chapter_title.ar: باب الْبُكَاءِ وَ الدُّعَاءِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – The weeping and the supplication during the Salāt

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/79)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/79)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَنْبَغِي لِمَنْ يَقْرَأُ الْقُرْآنَ إِذَا مَرَّ بِآيَةٍ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ فِيهَا مَسْأَلَةٌ أَوْ تَخْوِيفٌ أَنْ يَسْأَلَ اللَّهَ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ خَيْرَ مَا يَرْجُو وَ يَسْأَلَهُ الْعَافِيَةَ مِنَ النَّارِ وَ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is befitting for the one who recites the Quran that when he passes by a Verse from the Quran wherein is a question or a caution, he should ask Allah<sup>azwj</sup> during that for goodness he hopes for, and ask Him<sup>azwj</sup> for the well-being, from the Fire and from the Punishment’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama‘ah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One, when reading from the Holy Quran, should, upon coming across a verse that speaks of prayer or warning, ask Allah for what is good that one hopes for and for safety from fire and torment.’”
